What's up everyone? Okay, so I wanted to make a quick video today about how to handle customer service issues. And the reason for that is because a lot of people, one way or another, sometimes eventually will mess up. And that's because we're human and our businesses are not perfect. No one's is. And one of the things that's helped me a lot, and I got this from Richard Forch, who is an unbelievable business guy. He's a fourth generation business owner, same business, a furrier, okay, called Mano Swartz Furs. They do, I don't think he's even in the script, so I think we're fine. But they do like five million a year, inferred coats as a local business, okay, crushes it, alright. And anyways, I bring this up because he's taught me a lot of stuff over the years. But one of the ones that I really, really liked, I'll probably give you other ones he's given me, but is that, he's like, you know how we handle customer service issues? And I was like, how? He's like, because you can imagine, we've got women, we've got an event and the button's sewn on wrong or something, right? There's always something that can happen, right? He's like, there can only be one person who's upset. And I was like, what do you mean by that? He's like, there can only be one person in the angry book. And I was like, explain this to me. And so he's like, okay, let's say Mrs. Jackson comes in and she's like, this button was so wrong, it has a cross thing instead of a four way pattern. This is ridiculous. My jacket looks horrible, right? Because the pattern of the seam on the buttons instead of being a box is an X. And so he takes it and rather than being like, ma'am, no worries, not a big deal. We can fix it right now. Or if there's a tear that happens in the jacks, whatever it is, right? Instead of saying, oh, don't worry about it, it's not a big deal. Because then what that does is it invalidates that person's emotional response. It says, no, no, no, you shouldn't be angry. Let's calm down. But instead what he does is he tries to get more upset about the issue than the person is. And so he's like, you've got to be kidding me. He's like, they did a cross sectional instead of a box sew on your buttons. And they're like, yeah. He's like, did you go out in public? Did anyone see you with that? That's horrible. You must have been ashamed. Oh my gosh, this is terrible. How did this? I mean, you went in public with this? Like, oh my gosh, this is horrible. I'm going to find the employee who did this. So I will find them and figure out what exactly happened. And then all of a sudden, the employees, the clients, will start trying to bring them down. Bring them down. Be like, no, no, no, it's not that big of a deal. Like I'm sure it was just a small mistake. Like if you guys can just take care of it. And he's like, no, no, this is a huge deal. We're going to handle this right now. All right? And so take the jacket and I'll go in the back and I'll fix it. And be like, I am so sorry. What happens? They feel like they're totally validated in their response. And then they feel like the customer service was amazing. It also elevates the level of excellence that you portray as a business. That if something that small makes you that upset, then they can see what level of service you're providing throughout your entire business. I'm going to tell you a second story. And this one was one that happened. This is a real story. I'm going to try and change the names of the characters. But I had a girl named Amanda. Okay. Amanda was a client of mine in Huntington Beach and she had somehow, somehow not been put into the billing system. I don't know how she got it, whatever it was, but she came like three, four, five days a week for like five months. And one day I was like, Amanda, come here for a second. And she was like, what's up? I was like, you haven't been built. And I could immediately see in her eyes that it was that she knew that she wasn't being built. And I was like, okay, not a big deal. She's like, well, I can't afford this. And I was like, all right, I was like, that's fine. I was like, but you know, you still need to pay for the time you come. And I was like, so right now it's, it's, it's a thousand bucks is what you owe us. She's like, we don't have that kind of money. I was like, well, like you knew that you weren't getting built. Like you could have brought this up. And I, like I said, I don't know how it ended up happening, but it did. She was one of the morning people. So I wasn't there in the morning. A lot of times I was in the afternoon and sometimes whatever, it doesn't matter. And so what I said was this, and I recommend saying this. I was like, well, let's look in the system. Let's see who signed you up. I was like, okay, it was, it was John who signed you up. And she was like, yeah. And I was like, it's not a big deal. I was like, Gabby, all right. Well, you don't know her last name. I was like, all right, Gabby, don't worry about it. I'm just going to take it out of John's paycheck. I was like, we'll just take it over the next couple months. Okay. And that way everything's, everything's even. Okay. And she was like, no, no, you can't do that to John. And I was like, it's fine. I mean, someone has to pay for it. You know what I mean? It's like, you, you got a level of service and like, we're out of exchange. Like you need to pay, like payment needs to be made to the business. All right. And since he was the one who made the mistake for, you know, and has been, has been seeing you in the mornings and never thought to check and he was the one who signed you up, I could imagine that maybe he did this on purpose. Right. And, and I was like, it's totally okay. I was like, we'll just take it out of his paycheck. And she was like, no, you can't do that. And I was like, all right, well, then what do you want to do? And she was like, I will, I will, I will, I will figure it out. And, and lo and behold, she, she figured it out and she came in with the cash and she paid for it. So that's another way she uses this, but the angry boat story I just love because I think it's, it's just, it works with all sorts of situations, customer service wise. And it's just, it's just a really easy obstacle overcome. So I would use that if you are not using it already and using the trainers as a, as a sort of foil. And John, my trainer knew that I was doing, I told this, told it before I was like, I'm not going to take it out of your check, dude. But he, but he, like, he like played along for the, for the effect. So he was like, Hey, you know, Gabby, thanks so much for taking care of that because that would have been on me. And you know, so he just reinforced it. So use that to your advantage. I have another good one that I will share soon. All right. So hope that was just for you and I'll talk to you guys on the next episode.
